sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: KVC Westerlo are in poor form, winless in their last 5 matches with 3 losses and 2 draws, struggling defensively with no clean sheet in 4 games. KAA Gent, on the other hand, have shown mixed results, with 2 wins, 1 draw, and 2 losses in their last 5, but their attacking output remains strong, scoring in most recent games. Gent's recent heavy defeats highlight defensive vulnerabilities, though.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two encounters, KAA Gent have dominated KVC Westerlo, with a 4-1 victory earlier this season and a 2-2 draw in December 2024. Historically, Gent are unbeaten in their last 10 matches against Westerlo, and matches between these sides often feature over 2.5 goals with both teams scoring in 9 of the last 10 games.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: KVC Westerlo, sitting 12th with 15 points, have scored 19 goals (6th in the league) but conceded 24 (worst in the league), showing defensive frailties. KAA Gent, 6th with 21 points, have a balanced attack with 23 goals and a slightly better defense, conceding 22. Gent's higher big chances created and late-game scoring (9 goals after the 76th minute) could be decisive.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch
sections.text: For Westerlo, Nacho Ferri (4 goals) and Isa Sakamoto (3 goals, 3 assists) are key threats despite recent form dips. Gent's Omri Gandelman (7 goals, top scorer in the league) and Michał Skóraś (high rating of 7.45, 3 assists) are in excellent form and likely to influence the game significantly.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Outlook
sections.text: Westerlo will likely focus on leveraging their home advantage at Het Kuipje and exploiting set pieces, given their league-leading aerial duel win percentage (55.6%). Gent, with superior attacking stats and players in form, may dominate possession and look to score late, as their goal distribution suggests strength in the final stages.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At half-time, the scoreline between KVC Westerlo and KAA Gent remains goalless at 0-0. Both teams have shown moments of promise in the attacking third but have failed to convert their chances. Westerlo has had a slight edge in creating opportunities, with a couple of significant missed chances, while Gent has been marginally more composed in possession. The match is finely balanced, with neither side able to dominate consistently. Defensive resilience from both teams and a lack of clinical finishing have defined the first half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw fluctuating momentum between the two sides. Westerlo started strongly, showing intent in the opening minutes with sustained attacking pressure. Gent responded with a notable spell of dominance shortly after, pushing forward with purpose. However, Westerlo regained control for a prolonged period mid-half, mounting several threatening attacks. Towards the latter stages of the half, Gent fought back with a strong attacking surge, only for Westerlo to finish the half on top with a late flurry of activity in the opposition’s half. The momentum swings indicate an unpredictable second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key team statistics
halftimeSections.text: Looking at the half-time statistics, Westerlo has shown more intent in the final third, recording more entries and penalty area penetrations compared to Gent. They’ve also created more significant scoring opportunities but have been let down by poor finishing. Gent, on the other hand, has held a slight edge in possession and demonstrated strength in aerial duels, winning a higher percentage than their opponents. However, their attacking output has been less frequent, with fewer entries into dangerous areas. Both teams have been disciplined, with just one booking each.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Standout players
halftimeSections.text: For Westerlo, Doğucan Haspolat has been instrumental in midfield, dictating play and creating key opportunities for his teammates. Up front, Nacho Ferri has been a constant threat despite missing a big chance. On the Gent side, Wilfried Kanga has looked dangerous as the focal point of their attack, though he too squandered a notable opportunity. Tiago Araújo has been a creative force for Gent, setting up plays with precise passing. Both goalkeepers, Andreas Jungdal for Westerlo and Davy Roef for Gent, have been solid, contributing to the clean sheets so far.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Tactical observations
halftimeSections.text: Westerlo appears to be focusing on exploiting the flanks, using their wingers to stretch Gent’s defense and create openings in the box. Their approach has yielded chances, but they need to be more clinical in front of goal. Gent has adopted a more balanced strategy, maintaining possession and looking to build attacks through the middle. However, their lack of penetration in the final third suggests they may need to take more risks in the second half. Both teams could benefit from tactical tweaks to break the deadlock.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: The second half is likely to see an increase in intensity as both teams push for a breakthrough. Westerlo will aim to capitalize on their higher number of created chances and may focus on getting their key attackers into better positions. Gent, meanwhile, could look to leverage their aerial strength and possession play to create more sustained pressure. Given the attacking intent shown by both sides, it feels like goals are on the horizon if either team can find their finishing touch.
